reasoning: The bearish case rests on premarket doji exhaustion (05:55 bar = -0.01 body, <5%) and CLEAN_BEAR ribbon alignment, but premarket bars are thin-volume noise (05:55 is 3h40m before RTH open; volume is 5-10% of RTH). Price is currently $0.40 ABOVE Carry support (661.19) and $1.12 above the fast EMA (660.47)—no breakdown has occurred yet, only a setup-in-waiting. VIX rising to 25.87 is elevated but not panic (panic = 35+); post-FOMC repricing followed by stabilization into a zero-event day is historically common. XLE down only -0.53% suggests risk-off is contained, not cascading. The real bearish trigger requires breaking 661.19 with volume confirmation; without that break, price above pivot (660.54) gives bulls the structural edge. The consensus is preemptively bearish on an exhaustion pattern that may be invalidated within the first hour of RTH.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: SPY opens at 661.50 or higher and holds 661.19 support (Carry) on first dip within 15 min of RTH open (09:30-09:45 ET)
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Breaks the premarket exhaustion narrative and validates that the doji was thin-volume noise. If RTH opens firm above Carry and doesn't test down immediately, the BEAR_stack is just decoration; price above pivot = bull bias. Shorts covering at support.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: VIX drops from 25.87 to <24.0 within 30 min of RTH open
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Invalidates the 'fear is rising' consensus signal. Post-FOMC repricing can reverse sharply if UST yields stabilize or equity volatility shows stabilization buying. A VIX drop from 25.87 to sub-24 would indicate shorts taking profits and risk sentiment reset, supporting a bounce toward 663.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: XLK rallies >0.30% at RTH open (first 30 min) while SPY consolidates in 661-662 range without directional conviction
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Breaks the 'all-red rotation' consensus and proves narrow breadth was a short squeeze, not capitulation. Tech is the sector consensus leader; if XLK shows first strength, the risk-off narrative collapses and SPY follows higher into 663-664. This scenario isolates that internals' breadth call was premature.

weakest_consensus_link: Assumption that premarket exhaustion (05:55 doji, thin volume, 05:xx bars in general) predicts RTH momentum. Premarket bars are 5-10% of RTH liquidity and are poor predictors of open-hour behavior, especially after a major FOMC repricing overnight.
key_observations: Unanimous 4-of-4 bearish consensus is well-founded structurally: CLEAN_BEAR ribbon (144c spread), all sectors red, VIX rising, no bullish anchor.
However, price has NOT broken 661.19 yet—it's currently $0.40 ABOVE support. The consensus calls the setup in place but the trigger hasn't fired.
Premarket doji exhaustion at 05:55 is being treated as RTH-predictive, but premarket is thin volume; volume profile and momentum reset at 09:30 RTH open with 100x the liquidity.
VIX at 25.87 elevated but not panic (panic = 35+). Post-FOMC repricing into a flat-event day typically stabilizes, not cascades.
XLE down only -0.53% is constructive for risk-off narrative continuation; if energy bounced, risk-off would fail entirely. Narrow but not cascading breadth.
Zero macro events 2026-03-19 is BULLISH for shorts (no forced liquidation windows) but also bullish for longs (no fresh bad news catalysts; stabilization possible).
The consensus lacks ANY bullish perspective—no agent found a counterargument. This creates a blindspot for a whipsaw rally into 663 if shorts pile in at 661.19 and get stopped out.
